title: 9 Posting Advices, Account Statements, and Reports
body_text:
9	Posting Advices, Account Statements, and Reports
9.1	Overview of Posting Advices, Account Statements, and Reports
The reports described in this Chapter provide additional information that can assist with reconciliations. These reports arenГўВҖВҷt generated on CAS Online but are available on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. Refer to the File Exchange and Reports Distribution Part for procedures on how to access these reports. A description and a sample of each report is provided in this Chapter.
The following reports are available in the MERG file which can be downloaded from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. MERG files are located in the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs root directory. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder:
CHQ4 ГўВҖВ“ Credit/Debit Memo Slip
ADVC ГўВҖВ“ Credit/Debit Memo Slip
CHGS ГўВҖВ“ Banking Fees and Charges. For financial institutions in Ontario, this report is only available as a PDF in the Banking folder.
For financial institutions in BC, the STMT ГўВҖВ“ Statement report is located in the root directory of the File and Report Exchange and, if applicable, as a PDF in the Banking folder. For financial institutions in Ontario, a PDF of the STMT ГўВҖВ“ Statement report is located in the Banking folder.
9.2	Viewing Adjustment Reports
Central 1 provides adjustment reports that list manual transactions posted to Central 1 accounts such as adjustments and miscellaneous credit or debit memos. The CHQ4 ГўВҖВ“ Credit/Debit Memo Slip is generated for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs member financial institutions with a BC transit number. The ADVC ГўВҖВ“ Credit/Debit Memo Slip is generated for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs member financial institutions with a different regionГўВҖВҷs Transit Number.
The ADVC and the CHQ4 are similarly formatted. Where applicable, the reports contain supplementary information. Use this information to post general ledger entries or adjustments to membersГўВҖВҷ accounts. The Notes field contains the reference number and/or transaction details from the statement. See Figure 1 and Figure 2.
Figure 1: CHQ4, Credit/Debit Memo Slip
Figure 2: ADVC, Credit/Debit Memo Slip
9.3	Viewing Account Statements
The Monthly Statement report is an account statement that shows all debit and credit transactions settled for each calendar day of the month. It is produced during the night of the first Business Day of the month.
This report includes a description of each transaction and posting narrative. The reference column provides additional transaction details that can assist with account reconciliation and trace requests. The reference field contains a maximum of 16 digits (5 to 10 for the automated batch and up to 16 for the manual batch). The reference number can be matched to the reference number on the posting advice. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Sample Monthly Statement
9.4	Viewing Billing Reports
The Banking Fees and Charges (CHGS) report shows itemized banking fees and item charges. It is produced monthly and is available on the second Business Day of the month.
The report contains three sections: Fees, Item Charges, and Deposit Interest. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Banking Fees and Charges Report
The Fees section contains charges associated with providing a monthly service such as deposit and loan interest, and technology programs. For each fee type, the current and previous month fee is listed as well as the current month volume.
The Item Charges section is a detailed list of the individual billing charges associated with processing payment transactions volumes for the month. For each payment type, the current and previous month charge is listed, as well as the volume for the current month, previous month, and year to date. A subtotal is printed for this section.
The Deposit Interest section shows the amount of deposit interest earned, if any.
END
